Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay All Proceedings By Defendant Dean Luca Krause; Declaration Of Lisa S. Serebin In Support Of Stay
Off Calendar Oct-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHARAN HIGBEE, Esq. of McDowell Hetherington LLP (Tel: 510-628-2194) for Plaintiff;
Atty. KEVIN DWIGHT, Esq. of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP (Tel: 415-291-7464) for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Meyer Corporation U.S.;
Atty. JOSEPH A. CREITZ, Esq. of Creitz & Serebin LLP (Tel: 415-466-3090) for Defendant Dean Luca Krause;

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DEAN KRAUSE'S Motion To Stay All Proceedings.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

The Motion to Stay All Proceedings by Defendant Dean Luca Krause is granted. Federal courts have exclusive jurisdiction over ERISA claims. (29 U.S.C. 1132(e)(1).) Defendant Krause's Complaint in Dean Luca Krause v. Meyer Corporation U.S., Case No. 20-cv-6088-DMR, alleges ERISA based claims regarding the same subject matter. "It is black letter law that when a federal action has been filed covering the same subject matter as is involved in a California action, the California court has the discretion but not the obligation to stay the state court action." (Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804.) The federal action will determine whether the Golden Executive Bonus Arrangement repayment obligation violates ERISA. (RFJN ISO Motion, Ex. 1, 17-20.) Here, the Complaint seeks interpleader relief regarding a dispute between the Defendants about access to the annuity based on the repayment obligation under the Golden Executive Bonus Arrangement Agreement. (Complaint, 26.) Additionally, the Cross-Complaint by Defendant Meyer Corporation seeks a judicial declaration that Defendant Krause must comply with the repayment obligation if the Golden Executive Bonus Arrangement Agreement is valid. (RFJN ISO Motion, Ex. 2, 41-50.) Therefore, a resolution of the federal case may dispose of part of this action. Moreover, the opposition papers do not show that Defendant Krause filed the federal action to harass or that the federal action would create a conflict with this action. (See Farmland Irr. Co. v. Dopplmaier (1957) 48 Cal.2d 208, 215.) As a result, this action is stayed pending the outcome of Dean Luca Krause v. Meyer Corporation U.S., Case No. 20-cv-6088-DMR, in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
